title: Hieracium pruinale
taxonomy: Hieracium pruinale is classified as a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the aster family (Asteraceae). It belongs to the genus Hieracium and is also known as the pruinose hawk's-beard. The classification of Hieracium pruinale is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Asterales, Family: Asteraceae, Genus: Hieracium, Species: H. pruinale. This species is native to Europe. It is a perennial herb that grows in a variety of habitats, including woodlands and grasslands.

benefits: Hieracium pruinale, also known as downy hawkweed, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits. intro: Hieracium pruinale, also known as pruinose hawkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to Europe and western Asia. It is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium pruinale include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Hieracium pruinale is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is used to treat a range of conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems.
